source_documents: https://www.rbi.org.in (Master Directions on Frauds - Classification & Reporting, incl. Early Warning Signals)
category: Credit fraud - early-default / first-party fraud pattern (early warning signals)
risk_signals: Default within the first few instalments after disbursal
Loan proceeds diverted or withdrawn immediately rather than used for the stated purpose
Clusters of early defaults sharing an originator, product, or geography
analyst_reasoning: When a borrower never intended to repay, the loan defaults almost immediately and the funds are siphoned at disbursal. Early-warning-signal frameworks treat this pattern - especially in clusters around a common originator - as a fraud indicator distinct from ordinary credit stress.
investigation_steps: Flag and review all first-payment and very-early defaults as potential fraud
Trace end-use of disbursed funds for immediate diversion
Cluster early defaults by originator, product, branch, and geography to find organised activity
decision: Move early-default cases into fraud investigation, file reports where intent is established, and audit the common originator.
outcome: Undetected, early-default fraud recurs through the same channel; EWS-driven clustering is what exposes the ring.
lessons_learned: Very-early default plus immediate fund diversion signals intent, not misfortune
Clustering by originator/geography turns isolated defaults into a detectable pattern
Early-warning-signal review is a fraud control, not merely a collections trigger
